vulnerability.reference: Users with visual impairments cannot visually verify the source text, creating complete reliance on VLM output.
vulnerability.generated: Blind/low-vision users lack the visual ability to verify app-read text against physical reality, creating total dependence on VLM interpretations.

impact.reference: Physical harm if users follow malicious navigation or safety instructions.
impact.generated: Users follow maliciously altered vocal instructions, leading to physical danger, misdirection, or exploitation in public spaces.

dose_response.reference: Adversarial text manipulations exploiting VLM ambiguities enable vocalization of harmful instructions, which in accessibility tools exposes blind users to physical harm and psychological distress.
dose_response.generated: Vision-language models create an attack surface for adversarial text manipulations, which, when used by blind/low-vision individuals in public reading apps, triggers total dependence on unverifiable interpretations, leading to users following malicious vocal instructions and experiencing physical danger.
